reply_content: I hear you. From how I see it, I don’t think it’s that Ginny’s anger isn’t valid, I just don’t think she’s a well-written character. I love the show, but I think the writers worked too hard on making Georgia sympathetic (because she was a murderer and a neglectful teen mom) that they didn’t give enough nuance to Ginny’s character. She comes off as one-dimensional: a teenager who went through trauma and as a result, comes across as bratty with a victim mentality, even when it’s justified. It’s harder to sympathize with a character who you feel doesn’t have as much depth and charisma and nuance. That’s just my take though.
